name: Golden Honey Braid
recipeInstructions:
Makes 1 loaf
1. Combine 1/4 cup of the water, 1 tablespoon of the honey, yeast and saffron (if desired). Stir to dissolve yeast and let stand until bubbly, about 5 minutes.
2. Fit the processor with a steel blade. Measure flour, oil, remaining 3 tablespoons of the honey, and salt into the work bowl. Process until mixed, about 15 seconds.
3. Add yeast mixture to flour mixture. Process until blended, about 10 seconds.
4. Turn on the processor and very slowly drizzle just enough remaining water through the feed tube into the flour mixture so the dough forms a ball that cleans the sides of the bowl. Process until the ball turns around the bowl about 25 times. Turn off the processor and let the dough stand for 1 to 2 minutes.
5. Turn on the processor and gradually drizzle in enough remaining water to make the dough soft, smooth, and satin but not sticky. Process until the dough turns around the bowl about 15 times.
6. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Knead raisins into the dough (if desired). Shape into a ball and place in a lightly greased bowl, turning to grease all sides. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let stand in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
7. Punch down the dough. Divide it into 3 equal parts. Shape each part into a strand 20 inches long. Braid the strands loosely together and place on a greased cookie sheet or in a greased 2 quart ring mold or tube pan. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let stand in a warm place until almost doubled, about 45 minutes.
8. Preheat oven to 350°F. Bake until golden and loaf sounds hollow when tapped, 30-40 minutes. Remove immediately from the cookie sheet or pan and place on a wire rack. Brush honey over crust. Cool.
